Title: Interdependent iron and phosphorus availability controls photosynthesis through retrograde signaling

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Ascorbic acid (chloroplast-localized pool) linked to chlorophyll stay-green phenotype
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: AsA in chloroplasts buffers ROS and supports maintenance of photosynthesis-related gene expression and chlorophyll accumulation under Fe/P stress
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Not affected by Fe deficiency according to discussion
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Iron deficiency; phosphorus deficiency; combined −Fe−P; exogenous AsA
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for chloroplast AsA-dependent prevention of chlorosis and repression of photosynthesis genes under combined Fe and P deficiency
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pht4;4 mutants fail to stay green under −Fe−P and show reduced chlorophyll compared with wild type. 2. Genomic complementation restores the stay-green phenotype. 3. Exogenous AsA rescues −Fe+P chlorosis in wild type and vtc4 but not in pht4;4, showing chloroplast AsA transport is required. 4. pht4;4 mutants downregulate PRGs under −Fe−P and accumulate excess ROS under −Fe−P.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS candidate follow-up; knockout mutant analysis; genomic complementation; chlorophyll measurement; qRT-PCR of PRGs; AsA quantification; ROS quantification; exogenous AsA rescue
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No general growth penalty reported; mutant specifically loses stay-green protection under −Fe−P
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS using PRG expression PC1 and 1001 Genomes SNP data; RNA-seq context (GEO: GSE163190)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to improve maintenance of photosynthesis during simultaneous Fe and P deficiency by enhancing chloroplast antioxidant capacity; likely synergistic with VTC4/bZIP58 module
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PHT4;4 transports both phosphate and AsA, but the study supports AsA transport activity as causal for the phenotype; other plastid Pi transporters (PHT4;3/4;5/4;6) and Pi regulators (PHR1, SPX1) did not phenocopy pht4;4

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant; ROS scavenging; supports chloroplast redox homeostasis and maintenance of photosynthesis/chlorophyll under nutrient stress
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Final enzymatic step in the plant ascorbic acid biosynthesis pathway, producing AsA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in shoots under combined iron and phosphorus deficiency (−Fe−P); repressed under iron deficiency with phosphorus (−Fe+P)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Shoots; increased about two- to threefold under −Fe−P, decreased about twofold under −Fe+P
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Iron deficiency; phosphorus deficiency; combined −Fe−P; exogenous AsA context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes AsA accumulation and is required to prevent chlorosis under combined iron and phosphorus deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VTC4 transcript is specifically induced under −Fe−P but repressed under −Fe+P. 2. vtc4 null mutants remain chlorotic under −Fe−P, showing VTC4-dependent AsA biosynthesis contributes to the stay-green phenotype. 3. Exogenous AsA rescues chlorosis of vtc4 under −Fe+P, indicating VTC4 acts through AsA production.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; presented as required AsA biosynthesis enzyme and final-step enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; null mutant analysis (vtc4); chlorophyll measurement; AsA quantification; exogenous AsA rescue
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: vtc4 plants accumulated about 35% less AsA under control conditions but were not chlorotic in +Fe+P; no broader growth penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering AsA-supported maintenance of photosynthesis under combined Fe and P deficiency, likely most effective together with chloroplast AsA transport capacity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Induction of VTC4 under −Fe−P is partially dependent on bZIP58; figure model suggests effect could be direct or indirect

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Chlorophyll; indirectly also ascorbic acid-associated stay-green phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports photosynthesis by maintaining expression of photosynthesis-related genes and chlorophyll accumulation under nutrient stress
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Photosynthesis gene expression and chlorophyll accumulation; partial control of AsA biosynthesis gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of photosynthesis-related genes; positive regulation of VTC4 expression; repression of bZIP58 transcript by ROS/−Fe
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 32 photosynthesis-related genes (PRGs), including PSBTN, PHOTOSYSTEM II 5 kD, PTAC16; VTC4
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PRGs; partially promotes VTC4 expression under −Fe−P
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key regulator required to maintain PRG expression and chlorophyll accumulation; acts downstream of chloroplastic AsA/ROS signaling
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Iron deficiency; phosphorus availability; chloroplastic ROS (H2O2); AsA status
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely feedforward module in which bZIP58 promotes VTC4, which increases AsA and helps sustain bZIP58 expression by limiting ROS; not fully established
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly downregulated in shoots by −Fe+P; repression alleviated under −Fe−P; weakly affected in roots
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Shoots; downregulated by iron deficiency in a phosphorus-dependent manner; AsA prevents repression; H2O2 strongly represses expression
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Iron deficiency; phosphorus deficiency; combined −Fe−P; exogenous AsA; H2O2 treatment
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes chlorophyll accumulation and prevents chlorosis by sustaining photosynthesis-related gene expression; loss causes constitutive chlorosis and high ROS
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bZIP58 underlies a major GWAS/eQTL peak linked to coordinated expression of 32 PRGs. 2. bzip58 mutants show constitutively reduced PRG expression and chlorophyll, causing chlorosis regardless of nutrient condition. 3. bZIP58 directly activates at least some PRG promoters and is repressed by −Fe and ROS, while P deficiency and AsA alleviate this repression. 4. bZIP58 is partially required for VTC4 induction under −Fe−P.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS/eQTL mapping; knockout mutant analysis; genomic complementation; qRT-PCR of PRGs and VTC4; nuclear localization by GFP; in planta transactivation assay in Nicotiana benthamiana; chlorophyll and ROS measurements; JUB1 promoter-driven expression tests
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of bZIP58 causes constitutive chlorosis and elevated ROS; no yield data reported
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native genomic promoter in complementation; 35S promoter for GFP localization construct; JUB1 ROS-responsive inducible promoter for functional re-expression
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (GEO: GSE163190); GWAS using 727-accession leaf expression dataset and 1001 Genomes SNP data
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for engineering nutrient-stress-resilient photosynthesis; should be tuned to avoid ROS-mediated repression and may be combined with PHT4;4/VTC4 modules; stress-inducible expression may be useful
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AsA supplementation cannot rescue bzip58 chlorosis, placing bZIP58 downstream of AsA action
